themes.exampleQuote: Not just your normal sugary affair but elevated with unique flavor and texture.
Amazing stop for a date night or after dinner.
Gluten and nut free options are 🔥
The lines are long and do not move.
Awesome baked goods but really needs a quality/service check on coffee.

notable_quote: You can tell a lot of time and love went into crafting those recipes — they should be proud.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are consistently won over by the baked goods — the lavender cake, the brown butter blondie, the vegan options — but the coffee program draws real skepticism, with two independent reviewers flagging execution gaps ranging from missing ingredients to a macchiato served in an espresso cup.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight Pittsburgh creators filmed Butterwood this quarter. Butterwood filmed none of it back. With 527,000 TikTok plays driven entirely by outside voices — including a 165,600-play video from @thepittstopp calling it among the very best bakeries in the city — Butterwood sits at an unusual intersection: a place the city is actively evangelizing while the business's own Instagram has been dark for 514 days. That gap is what holds it at #2 in its family tier, trailing Allegheny Coffee & Tea Exchange, whose social momentum is the signal of the issue. The rank climbs when Butterwood starts claiming even a fraction of the creator energy already circling it.
tldr_read: 527,000 TikTok plays from other people's accounts, zero Instagram posts in 514 days — strong visual catalog and creator fit, dormant owned social. Ones to Watch, #2 in Pittsburgh Cafes.
tldr_meaning: The creator coverage is already doing the work of a much more active business; the ceiling here is determined by whether Butterwood ever steps into the signal that's already been built around it.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: The City Has Been Selling Butterwood For 514 Days. Butterwood Has Said Nothing.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram after 514 dormant days
playbook.action: Post once a week for four weeks — cakes only, no captions needed to start — to reactivate the account's distribution signal.

playbook.headline: Address the coffee program publicly
playbook.action: Two reviewers flagged coffee execution independently; one review cited no ice, no chai, and a mislabeled macchiato — acknowledging the standard resets reviewer trust.

playbook.headline: Formalize creator relationships already filming
playbook.action: 28 unique creators posted 30+ TikToks this quarter without any apparent coordination — a simple in-store signal or creator contact point could channel that into recurring coverage.
